repo_prior_art.cite: none; eid_lingam_direction_min_order_v1 is a non-Gaussian cumulant-direction result, not Gaussian-ANM score-Jacobian local geometry

published_prior_art.cite: rolland2022score supplies SCORE's population functional and zhu2023sample supplies a finite-sample guarantee, but neither gives the mirror-path C_m(delta)-to-Chernoff expansion

repo_prior_art.cite: none; no catalogue entry gives a likelihood order-error exponent indexed by a diagonal SCORE-Jacobian gap

published_prior_art.cite: chernoff1952measure gives the simple-product testing exponent; the incremental content is its calibration by the proposed mirror-path score-gap calculation

repo_prior_art.cite: none; no in-catalogue local causal-order decision limit for a Gaussian-ANM mirror path

published_prior_art.cite: hoyer2008nonlinear and peters2014anm give qualitative ANM asymmetry, while standard local-testing machinery yields this elbow once Theorem 1 is established
hoyer2008nonlinear identifies the linear-Gaussian reversible exceptional boundary
zhu2023sample analyzes SCORE's finite-sample guarantee and koehler2023efficiency concerns density-parameter efficiency, not this cross-fitted diagonal-Jacobian contrast
the change-of-measure algebra is standard; no cited work gives this notation for the proposed template
koehler2023efficiency concerns density-parameter score-matching efficiency, not primitive large-deviation conditions for this causal-order rule

novelty_flags.label: Proposition 2
novelty_flags.one_line: Its rare-event impossibility conclusion refutes an L4-only premise for a newly defined template but names no published estimator or workflow that makes the refuted exponent claim.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 4
soundness_flags.one_line: K_{+,n} and K_{-,n} are defined as the Hellinger-tilted likelihood-weighted mistake probabilities, so the displayed identity and its iff are a pure change-of-measure/definition unfold rather than a substantive characterization.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: Theorems 1–3 / honest_scope
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The frontier and moving-alternative elbow are only for the explicitly constructed two-node mirror Gaussian-ANM path, not uniformly over nonlinear ANMs.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: A pointwise local causal-order testing characterization under the stated mirror-path regularity conditions.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: Proposition 2 and Open-ended Question 1 / ass:jacobian-estimability
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: Feasible template claims condition on existence of cross-fitted diagonal score-Jacobian estimators with the declared training-random L4 rate; that premise is not claimed to imply exponent attainment.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: Sign consistency holds conditionally under r_n=o(sqrt(C_m(delta_n))); exact-exponent attainment requires additional exponential-stability conditions.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Demote Theorem 4's tilted identity to a preliminary algebraic lemma or definition, and replace it with a non-tautological primitive sufficient-and-necessary condition for a specified nuisance-training procedure.

Either tie the rare-event obstruction to a named published score-training workflow that claims L4-type control, or retain it only as a technical limitation of the new template rather than a negative-result contribution.
Extend the mirror calculation to a normalized smooth tangent class with uniform constants relating C_m(delta) and Chernoff information, or prove that no such C_m-only uniform frontier is possible.

recommended_next_step: Revise: remove the tautological tilted-loss theorem from the contribution set and repair or demote the untargeted L4-impossibility claim; then continue with the pointwise field-tier geometry kernel.
